description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rounded, bulbous form and a generally smooth but pitted surface. Its coloration is predominantly a muted pinkish-tan, with patches of lighter and darker tones, and several visible scuffs and natural blemishes. The stone features a few shallow indentations and protrusions, giving it a weathered, organic appearance. Some areas show rougher, granular textures, while others are more worn and smoother, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements. The overall mass and density are consistent with a solid rock, likely sedimentary in origin. This stone would be commonly found as a natural obstacle or decorative element in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas.
description: A rounded, pinkish-tan stone with a pitted, weathered surface and irregular shape, featuring natural blemishes and rough patches.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, smoothest face of the stone with subtle color variations and a few small scuffs.
description_view_1: This angle highlights a pronounced bulge and a rougher patch with visible indentations and a more irregular contour.
description_view_2: This side reveals deeper pitting and a more pronounced groove, with rougher texture and darker coloration.
description_view_3: This perspective shows a rounded edge with several small protrusions and a generally smoother surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and pitting with no regular pattern; natural blemishes and color variations are visible.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and pitted, with visible signs of weathering and minor erosion, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural landscaping elements but contrasts with paved or built surfaces; may be partially surrounded by grass or soil.
